title: Smashed Beef & Onion Hummus Bowl
summary: Crispy beef smash patties on a silky bed of hummus, topped with juicy tomatoes and punchy pickled jalapeños.

method.description: Pick the parsley leaves and finely slice the soft stems. Tip the beef mince, baharat, parsley stems and salt into a mixing bowl.
Very thinly slice or mandoline the onion, then stir it through the mince. Roughly portion the mixture into four balls — no need to shape them.
Chop the tomatoes into bite-sized chunks and place in a bowl with a pinch of salt. Let them sit to release their juices.

Drain the chickpeas, reserving a splash of their liquid. Tip the chickpeas, tahini and olive oil into a blender with some of the reserved liquid. Blitz until smooth, then season with salt and a splash of pickle brine to taste.Shortcut: Use good-quality ready-made hummus if you're short on time.
Place a frying pan over a medium-high heat. Get a sheet of baking paper and a heavy-bottomed pan ready.

Once hot, add a meatball to the pan. Cover with the baking paper and smash down with the heavy pan to flatten. Remove the paper and weight, then let it cook until golden and crusted. Flip and cook through on the other side. Transfer to a plate and repeat with the rest.
Toss the pickled jalapeños and olive oil through the tomatoes. Season with a splash of pickle brine. Toss through the parsley leaves just before serving.
Spoon the hummus onto plates and spread it out. Top with pieces of the smash patties and a big helping of the tomato-jalapeño salad.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
